_comment: Scenario 33: Non-COCO obstacle avoidance via PATH A (SAM + detector fusion). Exercises the full E5 pipeline: SimulatedSAMBackend generates class-agnostic masks, MaskClassAssigner fuses with detector classes, MaskDepthProjector back-projects to 3D voxels. Non-COCO objects (cubes, panels, pillars) get GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE class. Based on scenario 30 route and scene.

gt_class_map._comment: Ground-truth class map. Non-COCO obstacles use custom id 100 (mapped to GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE by PATH A). Person uses COCO class 0.

scenario.name: non_coco_obstacles
scenario.description: Flight through environment with non-COCO obstacles. PATH A (SAM + detector fusion) classifies unknown objects as GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE and populates the occupancy grid for avoidance.
scenario._comment_base_config: Hint for tests/run_scenario_cosys.sh — used when caller does not pass --base-config. Makes `./tests/run_scenario_cosys.sh config/scenarios/33_non_coco_obstacles.json --gui` work without any extra flags.

perception._comment_detector: Perception v2 (Epic #520) design: detector provides class labels only — SAM masks are the primary obstacle source. Switched from color_contour (ground-texture ghost cells) to YOLOv8 so the detector fires ONLY on real COCO objects (person, chair, couch, bush). Non-COCO obstacles (wall, pillar, cube) have no detector bbox, get GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE via MaskClassAssigner fallback, and are represented in the grid purely through PATH A voxels. Detector output also drives dynamic TTL cells for reactive avoidance of moving objects — static promotion is paused when voxel_input is enabled (see mission_planner.occupancy_grid.voxel_input).

semantic_projector._comment_sample_grid_size: Issue #629 — dense sampling for no-HD-map scenarios. Each SAM mask gets 16×16=256 back-projection probes instead of the legacy 4×4=16. Per-frame cost ~+0.8 ms (negligible); expected to raise PATH A obstacle-discovery rate enough for the drone to build usable grid occupancy before reaching unknown obstacles (scenario 33 is the 'no HD-map' validation case).

path_a._comment: PATH A enabled with real class-agnostic SAM via FastSAM ONNX (YOLOv8-seg architecture trained on SA-1B). Produces masks for every distinct object/surface in the frame regardless of COCO class. Prerequisite: run `bash models/download_fastsam.sh` once to fetch FastSAM-s.pt and export to models/fastsam_s.onnx (~50 MB, ~80 ms/frame CPU). MaskDepthProjector back-projects the masks via Depth Anything V2 into world-frame voxels; non-COCO obstacles (walls, pillars, TemplateCubes) get GEOMETRIC_OBSTACLE labels because they don't match any YOLOv8 detector bbox.

sam._comment_use_cuda: Issue #626 follow-up — FastSAM on CUDA runs ~10-15 ms/frame vs ~80-150 ms/frame CPU, which is the difference between MaskProj publishing at 10 Hz vs 0.13 Hz (measured scenario 33 run 2026-04-24_170802).  Safe on CPU-only machines: load_model()'s canary falls back automatically.

altitude_filter._comment: Issue #645 (#658) — drop voxels above max_z_m (sky misprojections from SAM masking distant background) and below min_z_m (ground patches with depth bias).  Run 2026-04-30_174147 trace analysis: 22% of voxels were above 6 m and 10% below 0.3 m — a guaranteed 32% of every frame was ghost.  World-frame Z; thresholds 0 = disabled.  Mission area obstacles top out at 5 m (pillars), drone flies at 5 m altitude, so anything above 6 m is sky.

mask_size_filter._comment: Issue #645 (#659) — drop SAM masks whose bbox area falls outside [min_area_px, max_area_px] BEFORE back-projection.  Run 2026-04-30_174147: SAM produced 41-47 masks per 1280×720 frame for ~7 spawned obstacles.  Tiny masks (<900 px ≈ 30×30) are texture features that produce scattered ghost voxels.  Huge masks (>200,000 px ≈ 22% of frame) are sky / ground regions that produce ghost walls.  Real obstacles in this scenario produce masks of ~1k-50k px depending on distance.  0 = disable bound.

diag._comment: Issue #612 voxel-position trace — JSONL of pose + bboxes + voxel world positions per batch. Consumed by tools/plot_voxel_trace.py. The runner overwrites trace_path with <scenario_log_dir>/path_a_voxel_trace.jsonl so every run is self-contained; keep trace_voxels=true to opt in.

cluster._comment: Issue #638 Phase 1 — voxel 3D clustering. eps_m=1.0 matches OccupancyGrid3D resolution (1m); nearby voxels link into instances. min_pts=5 rejects single-frame depth artefacts (DA V2 horizon contours, edge flickers).

tracker._comment: Issue #638 Phase 2 — cross-frame instance tracker. max_match_distance_m=3.0 covers per-frame cluster motion at scenario-33 cruise (drone @2 m/s × 100 ms tick + depth jitter). track_max_age_s=2.0 keeps tracks alive through brief occlusions (yaw past pillar) but drops stale ghosts.

occupancy_grid._comment_inflation_radius_m: Issue #645 — was 2.5 m, which created a closed ring of overlapping halos around the spawned obstacle cluster (pillar_01/wall_01/wall_02/couch_01/pillar_02 at coords (8-22, 10-14)).  Pairwise-gap analysis on the cluster: pillar_01↔wall = 0.03 m, wall↔couch = -2.6 m, wall↔bush = -0.4 m, pillar_02↔couch = -3.4 m — FOUR pairs fully blocked, no path WP2→WP3 through the cluster.  D* Lite gave up 108 times in run 2026-04-30_170604; drone hovered at WP2 and timed out to LOITER.  Reducing to 1.5 m reopens four gaps (pillar_01↔wall = 2.0 m, wall↔bush = 1.6 m, pillar_02↔wall = 2.8 m, pillar_01↔chair = 4.5 m) while still blocking the genuinely-too-close pair wall↔couch (5.4 m apart, will always block at any reasonable inflation — drone must go AROUND not through).  Drone is 0.6 m wide so a 1.5 m halo on a 0.5-m-radius obstacle gives 1 m of effective clearance, well above safety margin.
occupancy_grid._comment_max_static_cells: Issue #645 — was 500, capping the static layer at exactly the budget meant new voxel promotions were silently dropped once the outbound wake filled the cap.  0 = unlimited; with the Phase 3 instance-promotion gate (#642) blocking noise voxels at the source, raw cell-count is bounded by real obstacle volume rather than noise accumulation, so removing the cap is safe.  Empirical: drone collisions dropped 461→112 with all #645 fixes active but `Max promoted = 500` (= cap) — the cap is binding.  Lift it and see if the perception pipeline can keep up.

voxel_input._comment: PATH A voxel subscriber enabled — MaskDepthProjector batches from P2 feed the grid via the instance-promotion gate.
voxel_input._comment_static_cell_ttl_s: Issue #645 — re-enabled at 30 s after Phase 4 (#642) instance gate solved the noise problem.  Original #635 reasoning (DISABLED) was correct AT THAT TIME because per-cell promotion could not distinguish noise from real obstacles, so any TTL setting was a bad trade-off.  That constraint is now lifted: the Phase 3 instance gate (#641) blocks single-frame noise at the source, so anything reaching the static layer is a real tracked instance.  TTL now serves a different purpose: decaying STALE observations (real obstacles seen earlier but currently out of FOV) so the static layer reflects current sensor view, not lifetime accumulation.  Wake-along-path analysis on run 2026-04-30_171139 (path_a_voxel_trace.jsonl): 120 of 251 occupied cells along the return route from (42, 25) to (0, 0) were pure wake (not near any real obstacle) — the drone's outbound flight deposited a 2x-thick wall that blocked return.  At 30 s TTL: outbound voxels age out before the drone closes the loop; real obstacles re-observed every frame stay fresh forever.
voxel_input._comment_instance_gate: Issue #638 Phase 4 — instance-aware promotion. PATH A voxels carry instance_id from Phase 1's clusterer + Phase 2's tracker. Voxels with id=0 (noise — single-frame depth artefacts that didn't form a coherent cluster) are rejected outright. Tracked instances must be observed in 5 distinct frames (~0.5s @ 10Hz) before any of their voxels reach the grid. Real obstacles cluster across frames and promote; transient noise never accumulates. This is the architectural fix that PR #636's empirical journey proved was needed (see PR #639/#640/#641).
voxel_input._comment_position_clamp_m: Issue #645 — was 200 m, far too generous.  Trace analysis on run 2026-04-30_165441 (1747 batches, 6.2 M voxels) showed median voxel X = 42 m, p90 X = 43 m — a ghost wall ~20 m past the farthest obstacle in the mission.  DA V2 + reprojection produces depth-blowup voxels that pile up at the clamp's outer edge.  With voxel_on_target = 3.2 % (target ≥ 20 %), 97 % of cells in the static layer were ghosts, and the planner was routing AROUND ghosts rather than around real obstacles.  Mission area is X∈[0,25], Y∈[0,30], so 30 m gives a safe margin while killing the bulk of the depth-blowup ghost field.

mission_planner._comment_static_obstacles: No HD-map. Perception-driven avoidance via PATH A pipeline — SAM masks + depth → occupancy grid.

waypoints._comment: Fly between pillar and wall — forces tight avoidance.

pass_criteria._comment: Enforced by tests/run_scenario_cosys.sh Phase 6 (grep against combined.log). Validates the full PATH A pipeline is engaged: P2 SAM thread + MaskDepthProjector publisher and P4 voxel subscriber + grid writer. The log_must_not_contain list rejects the failure modes from the pre-#608 run (STUCK loop → LOITER escalation + silent PATH A init failure).
log_must_not_contain: [PathA] Requested via config but initialisation failed

checks.name: Radar first scan
checks._comment: CosysRadar backend successfully polled LiDAR data at least once.

checks.name: MaskClassAssigner active
checks._comment: MaskClassAssigner fused detector classes with SAM masks.

checks.name: Detector detections (non-zero)
checks._comment: Detector fired on the spawned/environment objects.

checks.name: Occupancy grid populated
checks._comment: At least one cell promoted from perception output.

checks.name: Avoider sees obstacles
checks._comment: Planner or avoider reacted to obstacles.

checks.name: VIO remained NOMINAL
checks._comment: Inverse check: zero DEGRADED events (Cosys ground-truth VIO should stay green).

checks.name: Mission completed (LANDED)
checks._comment: FSM reached LANDED — drone navigated the full waypoint loop.
